feature: Security and Code Quality Hardening (Jules Scan)
patterns_observed: Using child_process.exec is inherently risky when passing variables, even if the current implementation restricts those variables to a static array. Transitioning to execFile completely eliminates shell-based injection vectors.

Socket.IO servers default to blocking cross-origin requests, but setting origin: '*' removes this protection entirely. Explicitly defining allowed origins (like localhost ports) is crucial for securing local agent backends against cross-site WebSocket hijacking (CSWSH).

Initializing multiple MCP servers sequentially introduces an O(N) boot delay. Using Promise.allSettled allows concurrent connection attempts while still handling individual server connection failures gracefully without crashing the main process.
corrections: Replaced exec with execFile in EnvironmentProbe to mitigate command injection.
Restricted Socket.IO CORS policy from '*' to specific localhost origins.
Refactored sequential MCP server initialization into parallel promises.
Fixed overly permissive 'any' types in Agent mapping.
proposals: Implement an automated linting rule (e.g., ESLint security plugin) to block the use of child_process.exec codebase-wide, forcing developers to use execFile or spawn instead.
Extend CORS configuration to read dynamically from a centralized Config manager rather than inline environment fallbacks in server.ts.
